title: larrea tridentata subsp. glutinosa
intro: Larrea tridentata subsp. glutinosa, commonly known as the desert broom shrub, is a plant species found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This shrub has been used by indigenous cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Larrea tridentata subsp. glutinosa include reducing fever, alleviating arthritis symptoms, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and can be used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
